name: Spruce Log
description: A rough-cut log of spruce timber.

name: Vampire Plant
description: This rare variety of parasitic plant is known to suck the lifeblood from all flora with which it comes in contact.

name: Thavnairian Mistletoe
description: A variety of parasitic plant that is commonly found growing on the stout acacia trees of Thavnair. The inhabitants of Thavnair's capital city of Radz–at–Han have a strange custom that states if two people meet underneath a sprig of mistletoe, they must each give the other one of their shoes.

name: Prickly Pineapple
description:
A sweet and tangy fruit covered in a thick, spike-laden skin.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Prickly Pineapple Seeds
description: Prickly pineapple seeds suitable for garden cultivation.

name: Silkworm Cocoon
description: The lightweight cocoon of the elusive silkworm.

name: Trillium
description: A beautiful, white, tripetaloid flower.

name: Trillium Bulb
description: If planted and tended with loving care, this bulb should grow into a lovely trillium plant.

name: Kidragora
description:
Even in space, everyone can hear this seedkin scream.

Use item to acquire the kidragora minion.

name: Honey Lemon
description:
This rare type of lemon native to the continent of Othard is so sweet, it can be eaten raw.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Honey Lemon Seeds
description: Honey lemon seeds suitable for garden cultivation.

name: Dzemael Tomato
description: A rare crossbreed of ruby tomato developed and perfected by House Dzemael botanists.

name: Dzemael Tomato Seeds
description: Dzemael tomato seeds suitable for garden cultivation.

name: Black Truffle
description: A rare black mushroom that grows in the soil, rather than above it, making the specimen almost impossible to locate. It is treasured as a delicacy for its unique aroma.

name: Darksteel Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the metal darksteel.

name: Gold Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the precious metal gold.

name: Coblyn Larva
description:
Too dangerous to allow to grow any larger...too adorable to condemn to the furnace.

Use item to acquire the coblyn larva minion.

name: Volcanic Rock Salt
description: The red hue of this rock salt is said to come from the volcanic soil of O'Ghomoro.

name: Astral Rock
description: An uncut, unpolished stone imbued with an unbalanced elemental aspect tipping towards astral.

name: Gold Sand
description: Glittering sand consisting of tiny grains of gold.

name: Scarlet Sap
description: Its color reminiscent of blood, this sap is used in the working of aged lumber.

name: Apricot
description:
A variety of stone fruit borne over from the Near East. Its orange flesh is at once sweet and tart.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Blood Orange
description:
A rare variety of orange grown in La Noscea, named for its unusual red flesh.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Bamboo Stick
description: A cut and dried piece of thick bamboo.

name: Shroud Tea Leaves
description: Picked from plants that grow wild in the Black Shroud, these leaves are extremely bitter and are not ideal for brewing tea.

name: La Noscean Leek
description: A rare variety of leek, typically harvested young for a milder flavor.

name: Waterfowl Feather
description: Unlike those of tree-dwelling cloudkin, waterfowl feathers are exceptional at trapping in heat while keeping out water.

name: Young Cieldalaes Spinach
description: This bunch of Cieldalaes spinach has been picked young to reduce bitterness.

name: Ferberite
description: A jet black mineral with alluring luster.

name: Raw Iolite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be iolite.

name: Raw Topaz
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be topaz.

name: Native Gold
description: A high-purity, naturally occurring nugget of gold.

name: Raw Diamond
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be diamond.

name: Raw Emerald
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be emerald.

name: Raw Ruby
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be ruby.

name: Raw Sapphire
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be sapphire.

name: Grade 3 La Noscean Topsoil
description: Grade 3 topsoil obtained in the La Noscean region. Increases the likelihood that plants will yield higher quality crops.

name: Grade 3 Shroud Topsoil
description: Grade 3 topsoil obtained in the Black Shroud region. Increases the likelihood that plants will yield more crops.

name: Umbral Rock
description: A rock with an elemental aspect leaning heavily towards umbral.

name: Grade 3 Thanalan Topsoil
description: Grade 3 topsoil obtained in the Thanalan region. Increases the likelihood that plants will intercross with one another.

name: Fragrant Log
description: A pleasant-smelling log obtained in the Black Shroud.

name: Redolent Log
description: An unforgettably pleasant-smelling log obtained in the Black Shroud.

name: Antumbral Rock
description: A rock with an elemental aspect leaning unnaturally towards umbral.

name: Platinum Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the precious metal platinum.

name: Ebony Log
description: A rough-cut log of ebony timber.

name: Cypress Log
description: A rough-cut log of cypress timber.

name: Pumice
description: Volcanic rock riddled with countless tiny holes which make the stone remarkably lighter than it appears. Parchment makers rub the pieces on vellum to prepare the pages for receiving ink.

name: Rosemary
description: A savory herb treasured for its ability to enhance focus and concentration.

name: Virgin Basilisk Egg
description: While all eggs a basilisk lays contain traces of rare ore, the first egg laid in life is thought to contain the highest concentration, making it highly sought after by potion mixers and alchemists.

name: Rarefied Pyrite
description:
Though widely disparaged as fool's gold, there are certain obsessive miners who would trade sacks of genuine precious metal for this glorious hunk of iron and sulphur.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Chalcocite
description:
“If you can't recognize the beauty inherent in its orthorhombic crystal system, then we can't be friends.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Limonite
description:
Artistic collectors will swoon over the lustrous ochre pigment that can be produced from this seemingly unremarkable rock of iron ore.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Abalathian Spring Water
description:
Vanu shamans collect spring water to make the paste they apply to their beaks before rituals. Some Vanu shamans are very, very particular about their spring water.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Aurum Regis Sand
description:
When the aether of one's opus must flow unimpeded, one cannot skimp on the conductive component of one's ink.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Triphane
description:
The potential value of this uncut stone is obvious to the practiced eye. Especially if you love yellow.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Kyanite
description:
“If I can cut this just so, it will provide the perfect accent for a rather rakish turban I've been weaving...”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Star Spinel
description:
Rubies are red. Sapphires are blue. Star spinels are more of a violet, though, and that's the hue glinting from within this promising stone.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Azurite
description:
Be sure to don your swimming trunks before losing yourself in the deep, deep blue of this entrancing raw gemstone.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Silvergrace Ore
description:
“It's beautiful! Stunning! Magnificent! Now find me three more.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Titancopper Ore
description:
Never has titanium and copper existed in a more harmonious blend of minerals. It promises to make a wonderful teapot.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Petalite
description:
Not quite clear and not quite white, you're not quite sure why the jewel hiding in this rock is so valuable.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Lazurite
description:
One day, this unfinished gemstone of clearest blue could grace an elegant ring or an exquisite necklace or a face-smashing fist weapon. Possibilities!

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Sea Salt
description:
“Don't you dare throw that over your shoulder. Here, use the table salt.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Raw Onyx
description:
Even in its rough, natural form, the shimmering blackness of this jewel is almost dangerously captivating. So...pretty...

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Abalathian Rock Salt
description: Mined from veins found in Abalathia's Spine, this pinkish salt has a high mineral content which adds depth to its flavor.

name: Red Quartz
description: Several small pieces of deep red unpolished quartz.

name: Adamantite Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the rare metal adamantite.

name: Astral Moraine
description: Gravel with an unbalanced elemental aspect tipping towards astral.

name: Violet Quartz
description: Several small pieces of deep purple unpolished quartz.

name: Aurum Regis Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the rare metal aurum regis.

name: Lumythrite Ore
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be lumythrite.

name: Sun Mica
description: A decent-sized chunk of rock made up of what appears to be thousands of thin sheets of golden crystal compacted together into a single mass.

name: Blue Quartz
description: Several small pieces of deep blue unpolished quartz.

name: Red Alumen
description: A reddish variety of the mineral used by leatherworkers to tan animal hides. It lacks the deodorizing properties of its lighter counterpart.

name: Perlite
description: A strange, pearlescent volcanic rock that expands tenfold upon heating in a kiln or forge.

name: Dacite
description: A solid piece of cooled lava, its burning fury in a dormant state.

name: Pearl Sprouts
description: Tiny cabbage-like vegetables native to the highlands of Coerthas.

name: Coerthan Tea Leaves
description: Considerably smaller than Thanalan tea leaves, more than double the amount of Ishgardian tea leaves are required to brew a single cup of tea.

name: Chives
description: A thin green onion used in many Ishgardian dishes.

name: Pearl Sprout Seeds
description: Pearl sprout seeds suitable for garden cultivation.

name: Coerthan Tea Seeds
description: Coerthan tea seeds suitable for garden cultivation.

name: Old World Fig
description:
A symbol of long life and femininity, this autumn fruit was originally brought from their homeland to Eorzea by the Sharlayans.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Old World Fig Seeds
description: Old World fig seeds suitable for garden cultivation.

name: Porcini
description: A large fragrant mushroom popular in Coerthan cuisine.

name: Dravanian Mistletoe
description: A variety of parasitic plant found growing on trees in the Churning Mists.

name: Heavens Lemon
description:
Possibly due to the increased sunlight it receives growing above the clouds, heavens lemons lack the acidity of normal sun lemons.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Abalathian Mistletoe
description: A variety of parasitic plant found growing on trees in the Sea of Clouds.

name: Cloud Banana
description:
Though it resembles a tree, cloud bananas actually grow from an oversized herb. That does not, however, make them any less sweet.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Morel
description: This rare mushroom with an oblong webbed cap is highly sought after by culinarians the realm over.

name: Chysahl Greens
description: Though nearly identical in shape and color to gysahl greens, chysahl greens have a deeper, earthier flavor that chocobos (and man) seem to prefer.

name: Vanilla Beans
description: A sweet-smelling spice made from the dried pod of the rare vanilla plant. It is often used in the making of confectionaries as well as medicines.

name: Dravanian Paprika
description: A rare pepper that is more sweet than it is spicy.

name: Frost Cotton Boll
description: A soft and fluffy variety of cotton named for its resemblance to morning hoarfrost.

name: Almandine
description: A garnet-like mineral that, due to its extreme hardness, is used in grinding wheels.

name: Rhodolite
description: A particularly radiant uncut gemstone.

name: Seventh Heaven
description: A rare flower that has an elemental aspect tipped severely in the direction of astral.

name: Honeydew Almonds
description: A sweet variety of rare white almonds.

name: Snurbleberry
description:
This sweet, yet tart red berry is covered in tiny soft hairs that can be removed by lightly rubbing the fruit between one's palms.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Wattle Bark
description: This bark, when boiled down, produces large amounts of tannin─a substance used by leatherworkers to tan hides.

name: Old-growth Camphorwood Log
description: A glance at a cross section of this log reveals that a mere glance isn't enough to count the hundreds, perhaps thousands, of rings present.

name: Crystallized Sap
description: Tree sap that has crystallized and hardened into a small cluster.

name: Schorl
description: A blackish mineral possibly containing trace amounts of the precious stone tourmaline.

name: Smithsonite Ore
description: This slightly lustrous ore is characterized by its extremely high zinc content.

name: Meteorite
description: A glossy rock of sable black said to have fallen from the heavens.

name: Rarefied Reef Rock
description:
They say reef rock has been procured for restoration projects in a certain snowbound city. Perhaps this perfectly porous piece is destined for a structure of “holey” importance.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Tungsten Ore
description:
A truly precious find for one invested in the finest facets of tungsten-based fabrication.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Gyr Abanian Alumen
description:
“Well, tan my hide! You must have spent weeks in the mountains of Eastern Aldenard looking for this stuff.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Chestnut Sap
description:
Rather than experiment with amusing applications for this superior sticky substance, it's perhaps best to relinquish it to a tree sap professional.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Chestnut Log
description:
Gazing upon this rough-cut log, you cannot help but wonder if its lustrous lumber is destined for a valiant knight's lance or a bilge-bottomed buccaneer's bar stool. Anyone's guess, really.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Chestnut Branch
description:
“Aye, 'twould make a fine sentinel's longbow. But see, I have this prior commission to craft a fishing rod. N-No, that's not my penmanship!”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Chestnut
description:
A champion among chestnuts, predestined for the summit of a Sohm Al tart.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Chestnut Resin
description:
The viscous lifeblood you extracted from a vigorous yet defenseless chestnut tree. You monster.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Torreya Branch
description: The thick branch of a torreya tree.

name: Tungstite
description: A bright-yellow mineral formed by the weathering of scheelite.

name: Zeolite Ore
description: A porous silicate that adsorbs poisons, making it invaluable in myriad alchemical and medical concoctions.

name: Luminium Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the precious metal luminium.

name: Teak Log
description: A rough-cut log of teak timber.

name: Star Cotton Boll
description: A rare variety of cotton which produces nitid fibers that, when struck by light, coruscate like the very stars in the heavens.

name: Brown Mushroom
description: An earthen fungus that can commonly be found sprouting beneath trees growing in the Churning Mists.

name: Noble Sage
description: A fragrant herb used in medicines, perfumes, and cooking. Its deep blue flowers set it apart from its cousin, clary sage.

name: Raw Triphane
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be triphane.

name: Raw Star Spinel
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be star spinel.

name: Raw Rhodonite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be rhodonite.

name: Pummelite
description:
This oddly named ore was dubbed such by early Gyr Abanian settlers who believed the chunks of colorful mineral to be pieces of a larger rock shattered by a single blow of the Destroyer's fist.

※Not included in the gathering log.

name: Raw Imperial Jade
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be imperial jade.

name: Raw Azurite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be azurite.

name: Ala Mhigan Salt Crystal
description: These pinkish crystals grow naturally on the shores of Loch Seld.

name: Chromite Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the metal chromium.

name: Gyr Abanian Spring Water
description: Cold, clear mountain water harvested from hidden springs located in the highlands of Gyr Abania.

name: Palladium Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the metal palladium.

name: Windtea Leaves
description: When steeped in boiling water, the leaves of this rare perennial change color, turning a bright bluish green.

name: Countess Tea Leaves
description: Once consumed exclusively by the upper classes but now available to the hoi polloi.

name: Doman Yellow
description: A soft, clay-like soil believed to be formed of compacted yellow dust carried on the wind from faraway lands.

name: Bamboo Shoot
description: If removed from the ground when less than a fulm in length, bamboo shoots can be peeled and the fleshy interior eaten. A popular ingredient in Far Eastern stews and stir-fries.

name: Othardian Plum
description:
A greenish pink plum common across the eastern continent of Othard.

EXP Bonus: +3% Duration: 30m
(Duration can be extended to 60m by consuming multiple servings)

name: Hallowed Basil
description: A herb with an aroma so heavenly, the gods themselves must have blessed it (or so say merchants who would see you buy their stock).

name: Lotus Root
description: Though they appear solid from the outside, the cylindrical root nodules of the lotus flower are riddled with holes, possibly to accommodate for the waterlogged environments in which the plant grows.

name: Jhammel Ginger
description: Jhammel ginger's bulbous, aromatic roots can be sliced fresh and added to stews and seafood dishes to remove gaminess, ground and steeped in hot water to cure head colds, or dried and ground into a powder to use as a spice for breads and cookies.

name: Rhalgr's Streak
description:
Also known as Rhalgr's staff, this long-leafed medicinal herb is native to the highlands of Gyr Abania.

※Not included in the gathering log.

name: Torreya Log
description: A rough-cut log of torreya timber.

name: Rhea
description: A variety of ramie better suited to the climes of Far Eastern Othard.

name: Azim Cotton Boll
description: The white of Azim cotton is so bright that some compare it to staring at the sun itself.

name: Nightsteel Ore
description: Iron ore that has been exposed to the waters of a mineral spring, and undergone significant changes to both color and chemical makeup.

name: Hawk's Eye
description: A blue-grey jewel consisting of many colored layers. When polished, it resembles the piercing eye of a raptor.

name: Silvergrace Ore
description: A sizable piece of rock containing traces of a precious metal known as silvergrace.

name: Azim Spring Water
description: Water of uncommon clarity sourced from underground springs on the Azim Steppe.

name: Black Willow Log
description: A rough-cut log of black willow timber.

name: Hardened Veteran Tree Sap
description: A partially congealed ball of sticky sap extracted from an aged tree.

name: Hingan Flax
description: A distant cousin to the flax found in the highlands of Coerthas, the stalk of this fibrous plant is used to create a yarn known in Hingashi as “true linen.”

name: Gyr Abanian Ore
description: A naturally occurring chunk of lodestone.

name: Evergleam Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing a rare natural alloy of iron and chromium.

name: Urunday Log
description: A rough-cut log of urunday timber.

name: Yanxian Cotton Boll
description: These whimsical cotton flowers, found only in Yanxia, glimmer faintly like stars in the night.

name: Gale Rock
description: As the name suggests, this variety of stone is more strongly aspected than typical wind rock.

name: Water Crystal
description: A crystalline manifestation of aetheric water energy.

name: Solarite
description: A deceptively plain stone that, when cracked, momentarily erupts with bright light.

name: Shade Quartz
description: Several small pieces of dark unpolished quartz.

name: Raw Hematite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be hematite.

name: Raw Diaspore
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be diaspore.

name: Raw Lazurite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be lazurite.

name: Raw Triplite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be triplite.

name: Raw Petalite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be petalite.

name: Raw Onyx
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be onyx.

name: Prismstone
description: This peculiar type of stone shines with all the colors of the rainbow.

name: Beryllium Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the precious metal beryllium.

name: Tungsten Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing the metal tungsten.

name: Bog Sage
description: The azure flowers of this plant brighten the landscape when in bloom, but its true value is in its leaves.

name: Sweet Marjoram
description: A herb cultivated for both its medicinal uses and pleasant fragrance.

name: White Clay
description: Crumbly balls of white clay added to soil to improve both moisture retention and drainage.

name: Broad Beans
description: These large, spring green beans are easy to cultivate, and have served as a staple food for millennia.

name: Peppermint
description: This refreshing herb is suitable for use in cooking, essential oils, and tea.

name: Russet Popoto
description: An easy-to-peel variety of popoto with thin skin and a high starch content.

name: Mist Spinach
description: A highly nutritious, deep-green vegetable.

name: Lemonette
description: A tiny lemon replete with sour juice.

name: Sandalwood Log
description: A rough-cut log of sandalwood timber.

name: Sandalwood Sap
description: A partially congealed ball of sticky sap extracted from a sandalwood tree.

name: Ethereal Cocoon
description: As difficult to lay hands upon as bubbles on the water, from these cocoons can be spun a rare type of silk.

name: Brashgold Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock rich in a highly durable, naturally occurring alloy.

name: Fireheart Cobalt
description: Seasoned miners will recognize this as a type of sulfide, its composition lending its surface a permanent sheen that makes it look like a glowing ember in the light.

name: Purpure Shell Chip
description: A shiny piece of Purpure carapace.

name: Merbau Log
description: A rough-cut log of merbau timber, bearing the wood's distinctive scent.

name: Tender Dill
description: A small variety of dill characterized by its sweet fragrance. Finds use in both medicinal and culinary applications.

name: Imperial Fern
description: Presumably named by one of history's many oppressed lower classes, this fern's appellation cannot but refer to the pretentious way in which it curls.

name: Rarefied Larch Log
description:
You wonder if the fact that “larch log” is fun to say has anything to do with its inherent collectability.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Larch Sap
description:
Larch...sap. No, not nearly so fun to say as “larch log” but apparently just as collectable.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Shiitake Mushroom
description:
Only the finest fungi are foraged for fancy Far Eastern feast tables.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Pine Resin
description:
Unsurprisingly, this exceptionally gooey mass is a prime ingredient in certain beetle-based adhesives.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Pine Log
description:
Almost unnaturally straight and true, this log will hopefully be processed into lumber for a future masterpiece of carpentry...and not thrown into some chilly lordling's fireplace.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Pixie Apple
description:
One bite of this almost unbearably sweet fruit will have you flitting about like a pollen-drunk pixie.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Miracle Apple Log
description:
The real miracle here is that any of these trees were left standing after a sudden and inexplicable surge in the demand for applewood staves.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied White Oak Log
description:
A difficult material to work with, any artisan would nevertheless be eager to craft an enduring handle or lasting piece of furniture from this marvelous hunk of hardwood.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Sandteak Log
description:
After being carved and then covered with exquisitely woven wool, this attractive lump of timber could serve as the elegant display stand for some staggeringly expensive─and rarely worn─neckwear.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Amber Cloves
description:
Once dried and enshrined in a sturdy jar, these flavorsome flower buds will take pride of place on a master chef's spice shelf.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Kelp
description:
“Yes, an oriental breakfast is generally a simple affair, but that doesn't mean one should settle for any old seaweed in the sea.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Coral
description:
“This is it! The perfect piece of coral to complement my perfect coral butterfly. My aquarium is complete!”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Urunday Log
description:
Compared to your average cut of timber, the quality of this log is the difference between urunight and urunday.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Ashen Alumen
description: Unusually dark-hued alumen.

name: Duskblooms
description: A flowering plant with tiny purple blooms.

name: Hard Water
description: To the layman, hard water may sound like a convoluted way to say “ice,” but experts would disagree. In any case, it is at least soft enough to be poured into and out of a bottle.

name: Solstice Stone
description: A mysterious crystal of purest white.

name: Dolomite
description: A semi-translucent rock containing the rare mineral dolomite.

name: Wattle Petribark
description: Though this bark has a number of uses, being in an apparently semi-fossilized state does make it somewhat troublesome to work with.

name: Silver Beech Log
description: A rough-cut log of silver beech timber.

name: Raindrop Cotton Boll
description: The cotton produced by this particular species is said to be as delicate to the touch as a raindrop. Unfortunately, this makes it rather difficult to pick without crushing the boll.

name: Lunar Quartz
description: A grey rock tinged with a ghostly green hue.

name: Earthen Quartz
description: A rock with an elemental aspect leaning heavily towards earth.

name: Ghostly Umbral Rock
description: A rock with an elemental aspect leaning unnaturally towards umbral.

name: Rarefied Raw Ametrine
description:
This multicolored beauty was born from the fusion of amethyst and citrine.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Sharlayan Rock Salt
description:
“Everyone talks so much about how salt tastes and not enough about how it looks.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Bismuth Ore
description:
This mineral may look like a shiny rock, but when oxidized, it becomes a very shiny rock.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Phrygian Gold Ore
description:
Not everything that glitters is gold─sometimes it is much more valuable.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Eblan Alumen
description:
No one would call this alumen “rarefied” were it overabundant in the frozen north─certainly not for marketing purposes. Perish the thought!

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Blue Zircon
description:
The brilliance of this sample's blue hue remains nigh unparalleled in the natural world─particularly now that it has been removed from it.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Annite
description:
Handling may cause this mineral to fracture or flake, so best not. What do you mean, “too late”?

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Pewter Ore
description:
Pewter? I barely know her!

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rime Dolomite
description: This sample displays the particular dolomitization characteristic of the frozen wilds of Ilsabard.

name: Raw Eblan Danburite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be Eblan danburite.

name: Prime Siderite
description: A ferrous meteorite in apparent flux. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Siderite
description: This lucky siderite specimen has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Lunar Adamantite Ore
description: Ore rich in lunar adamantite.

name: Rhodium Sand
description: Sand consisting of tiny grains of rhodium.

name: Ewer Clay
description: Clay said to have been used to craft Thaliak's very own ewer.

name: Sophora Roots
description: Tendrils of a perennial plant replete with earth aether.

name: Palm Chippings
description: Bark from a Thavnairian palm tree.

name: Rarefied Palm Log
description:
The lack of rings makes this lumber impossible to date─or, as particularly aggravating naturalists might say, “stuck in the frond zone.”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Coconut
description:
Though this deceptive drupe does not taste like cocoa and it is not a true nut, culinarians tend to overlook these offenses in light of its deliciousness. It is on thin ice with the botanists, however.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Red Pine Log
description:
While this wood is more red-adjacent than truly red, “red-adjacent pine” does not exactly roll off the tongue.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Sykon
description:
A succulent symbol of vitality, this fruit pairs nicely with some honey and stone cheese.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Elder Nutmeg
description:
This sweet-scented spice is proven to help medicine go down. Consuming an entire spoonful, however, is not recommended.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Rye
description:
This hearty grain is a culinarian's delight─though much more so after the tiresome grinding step is complete.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Iceberg Lettuce
description:
This crisp, refreshing vegetable is often thought to be lacking in flavor, but those who enjoy the flavor of water doubtless find it absolutely overpowering.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied AR-Caean Cotton Boll
description:
These bolls are so irresistibly soft that some folks can't help but pluck them and pop them in their ears. Caution: under no circumstances should AR-Caean cotton be placed near the ears.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Mempisang Log
description: A rough-cut log of mempisang timber.

name: Golden Cocoon
description: These shining cocoons are woven only by Thavnairian silkworms.

name: Thavnairian Corn
description: A selectively bred variant cultivated from the maize once brought to Thavnair from the New World. Valued for its nutritional value and alchemical uses alike.

name: Double-edged Herb
description: Incredibly potent, but also incredibly hard to find without getting lost in the woods and meeting an untimely end.

name: Prime Crystalbloom
description: This sap-coated plant has taken on a glossy, crystalline appearance, but it is yet in flux. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Crystalbloom
description: This peerless crystalbloom has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Raw Rutilated Quartz
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be rutilated quartz.

name: Raw Zoisite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be zoisite.

name: Ilmenite
description: A hunk of moon ore rich in titanium.

name: Prime Achondrite
description: A meteorite primarily comprised of olivine and pyroxene. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Achondrite
description: This superior chunk of achondrite has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Ash Diatomite
description: Soil rich in diatoms and cultivated in Labyrinthos to superior quality.

name: Prime Chloroschist
description: A crystalline sample cultivated in the exacting environment of Labyrinthos. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Chloroschist
description: This glorious chunk of chloroschist has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Paldao Log
description: A rough-cut log of paldao timber.

name: Prime Sphongos
description: A spongy mushroom imbued with fascinating life energy. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Sphongos
description: This magnificent sphongos has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Haritaki
description: A Thavnairian nut often used in dyeing and tanning practices.

name: Prime Haritaki
description: A lustrous leaf of the plant born from the popular Thavnairian nut. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Haritaki
description: This spectacular sprig of haritaki has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Bayberry
description: A small branch of wax myrtle clipped from a shrub in Labyrinthos.

name: Mornveil Tree Bark
description: The quality of this bark is undeniable and likely due to the hospitable surrounds of Labyrinthos.

name: Maghemite
description: Originating in Thavnair, this iron oxide is often utilized as a pigment for its rusty red hue.

name: Softsilver Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing a naturally occurring mix of silver and quicksilver.

name: Prime Fossilized Dragon's Scale
description: This remnant of ancient draconic life was unearthed in Ultima Thule. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Fossilized Dragon's Scale
description: This uncanny fossilized dragon's scale has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Raw Spodumene
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be spodumene.

name: Field Mustard
description: The oil extracted from the seeds of this vivid yellow flower has various practical applications, not the least of which is as a reagent for curing skins and hides.

name: Lovingbloom
description: A prize “flower” grown in the nightmare fungal garden of Lovingway's Darlings.

name: Dark Cherry Log
description: A rough-cut log of dark cherry timber.

name: Prime Kukuru Beans
description: This bean is born of a kukuru tree cultivated within Labyrinthos's precisely controlled climate. Under the right conditions, it could become sublime.

name: Sublime Kukuru Beans
description: This wondrous kukuru bean has gone from prime to sublime through a process you don't understand and aren't going to worry about. What's important is that it promises an impressive yield from aetherial reduction.

name: Electrocoal
description: An ore containing trace amounts of electrope.

name: Brightwind Ore
description: Prolonged exposure to wind and sunlight has allowed this stone to absorb a great deal of aether.

name: Levin Quartz
description: Unpolished stone imbued with aetheric lightning energy.

name: Goldbranch
description: Though it appears withered at first glance, this glimmering branch is very much alive─or rather, it was, before being torn from its natural environment.

name: Volcanic Grass
description: Raised in nutrient-rich volcanic loam, this rare herbage is densely packed with aether.

name: Calamus Root
description: Rootstalk thrumming with strong lightning energy.

name: Rarefied Raw Dark Amber
description:
The gemstone of choice for dark goldsmiths.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Titanium Gold Ore
description:
There's gold in these hills! Titanium gold, that is.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Magnesite Ore
description:
In addition to its crafting purposes, this ore can be used to deter burglars, provided your aim is true.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied White Gold Ore
description:
For those who seek to smelt only the whitest and goldest of white gold.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Ra'Kaznar Ore
description:
The perfect smithing material for someone with a penchant for black and purple color schemes.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Ash Soil
description:
This faintly warm mixture of ash and soil was harvested from an active volcano by a true daredevil of a gatherer.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Mountain Flax
description:
With this, perhaps you can craft yourself a nice pair of hiking boots to replace the ones you wore out climbing this mountain in the first place.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Sweet Kukuru Bean
description:
A true chocolate connoisseur will accept no less than this, the sweetest of all kukuru beans, for their sinfully indulgent sachertortes.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Dark Mahogany Log
description:
“Bring me the darkest mahogany you have! Darker, I say! Darker!”

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Acacia Bark
description:
Used in the tanning of the finest quality hide for a leather so durable, the poor gargantua may one day forgive you for slaying it.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Acacia Log
description:
Take this to a skilled carpenter to be processed, and by day's end, you could be the proud owner of a first-rate fork.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Rarefied Windsbalm Bay Leaf
description:
A tisane brewed from this exceptional bay leaf would afford a weaver enough energy to sew nearly three dozen muffed mets. Enjoy in moderation.

※For collectable submissions only.

name: Turali Alumen
description: Quarried from the peaks of Urqopacha, this colorful mineral is prized by leatherworkers for its use in tanning hides.

name: Fine Silver Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing large quantities of silver.

name: Harmonite Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing iron mixed with traces of nickel and cobalt.

name: Alexandrian Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing metal composites unique to Alexandria.

name: Ipe Log
description: A rough-cut log of ipe timber.

name: Nopaliflower
description: A vibrant flower harvested from a cactus in the Shaaloani wilderness.

name: Blackseed Cotton Boll
description: These fibrous cotton flowers originated in ocean waters long forgotten.

name: Optical Fibergrass
description: The fibrous tendrils that form this peculiar grass have exceptional light-transmitting properties.

name: Raw Rhodochrosite
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be rhodochrosite.

name: Octahedrite
description: A chunk of mineral not dissimilar to the meteorites that litter the forest floor in Yak T'el.

name: Fulgurite
description: A chunk of vitrified minerals formed by a lightning strike.

name: Desert Lapis Ore
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be desert lapis.

name: Cordia Log
description: A rough-cut log of cordia timber.

name: Cordia Sap
description: A partially congealed ball of sticky sap extracted from a cordia tree.

name: Shaaloani Coffee
description: These rich, dark-roasted coffee beans are often used as a tanning agent.

name: Cochineal Pigment
description: A vivid red pigment is derived from the tiny insects that cluster upon nopales to feed.

name: Rose Garnet Ore
description: An uncut, unpolished stone containing what appears to be rose garnet.

name: Dense Aluminum Ore
description: A decent-sized piece of rock containing traces of highly dense aluminum.

name: Windspath Water
description: Filled to the brim with pleasantly smooth, almost entirely mineral-free water.

name: Carnauba Leaf
description: A palm frond containing a naturally occurring wax used by all manner of artisans.

name: Urqopacha Flax
description: Highly sought fibers sourced from a mere handful of locations in Urqopacha.

name: Fulgurpine Log
description: A rough-cut log of fulgurpine timber. Caution is advised when handling due to the potent lightning energy suffusing its fibers.
